tweets.text:
The Treaty of Paris desk is where peace commissioners concluded the American Revolution, securing independence for the United States in September of 1783. It traveled recently to be part of the new TV series “Franklin”.

📽️Video courtesy of Apple https://t.co/06tUFVwAmd

This week at State, we launched a new music diplomacy partnership with @YouTube, hosted the first-ever policy convening on LGBTQI+ rights and foreign policy, and honored individuals working to combat human trafficking. https://t.co/XUtu4mmzup

Most Americans have their own immigration story of relatives and ancestors who overcame incredible odds to build new lives and contribute to the fabric of America. During National Immigrant Heritage Month, we celebrate the contributions and achievements of immigrants in America. https://t.co/7bHNKFxld2

We’re celebrating all things #Pride this month. Do you know your history? Take our latest quiz to test your knowledge of LGBTQI+ U.S. milestones that shaped the movement and changed history. 🏳️‍🌈https://t.co/KNvNr9O7xb
Promoting respect for the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons around the world is central to the work we do. https://t.co/UyfYeZJXyJ

Artificial intelligence is transforming diplomacy. The State Department will use AI in a safe, responsible way to speed up our analysis, help in negotiations, and combat disinformation. https://t.co/3sNerZPQ3g

The choices we make now will define how technology is used, how it’s deployed, and to what effect, for a long time for the future. — @SecBlinken on the responsible use of Artificial Intelligence. https://t.co/CMi60RhRBn

Before the #T20WorldCup, we celebrated how more Americans are getting into cricket 🏏. Young cricketers taught @DepSecStateMR the right way to bowl, we heard from @WSHFreedom cricketer Obus Pienaar, and fans celebrated how the global sport brings people together. https://t.co/5xh8PmIbZ7
We’re committed to digital solidarity with those who share our vision for a vibrant, open, and secure technological future. https://t.co/odGHEqzyhH
.@SecBlinken participates in an “AI at State” conversation at the Department of State. https://t.co/mfoiKcGjik
.@SecBlinken hosts a Pride Month reception at the State Department. 🏳️‍🌈 https://t.co/NCLBFWdhV4  https://t.co/lZh7YJa0oG

Today, @SecBlinken hosted the State Department's Convening on U.S. Foreign Policy and LGBTQI+ Persons to highlight interagency work to advance LGBTQI+ equality and promote democracy, national security, and economic prosperity. https://t.co/jtpjsKelUK

.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at a room dedication and naming ceremony in recognition of Secretary Colin Powell and Secretary Madeleine Albright’s extraordinary service at the State Department. https://t.co/WEzMY35faZ  https://t.co/glU5kV5lfd
.@SecBlinken hosts an LGBTQI+ Foreign Policy convening at the State Department. https://t.co/KqPhnMt3qB https://t.co/zS55WRqDW2

Human trafficking is the definition of a problem no nation can solve alone. More than ever, we are working with more partners, including citizens and survivors, to understand and confront this complex challenge. https://t.co/9T4v0bi8UK

Freedom of religion is a bedrock American belief. Every year, @StateIRF chronicles the state of religious freedom around the world. We highlight both progress and shortcomings, with the aim to protect this human right for all. https://t.co/Zl6kHisTqF https://t.co/2pIQfz5wEg
.@SecBlinken and @IRF_Ambassador Rashad Hussain release the 2023 International Religious Freedom Report in front of partners, survivors, and civil society representatives.  https://t.co/irUQ3p6c7o

We’re taking action to minimize the very real effects of the climate crisis. Last year, the U.S. Mission to Japan, including @USEmbassyTokyo, completed the shift to 100% carbon-free electricity. Learn how and why they went green: https://t.co/j99b7SsmZ0

We played "Guest that Song" with @ECA_AS Satterfield. 🎶 🎧 Can you name these artists?

Here's a hint... It might have something to do with our U.S. Global Music Ambassadors — the State Department's new partnership with @YouTube! https://t.co/JPraU9XGea
.@SecBlinken Delivers Remarks At The 2024 @SelectUSA Investment Summit in National Harbor, Maryland. https://t.co/9hye1SOOM4

Since 2004, we have recognized over 170 individuals who have dedicated their lives to #EndHumanTrafficking. In honor of the 20th anniversary of the Trafficking in Persons Report Heroes program, learn more about the actions you can take to join the mission:https://t.co/FN1fgjaSbU
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks and participates in an U.S. Global Music Ambassadors event. https://t.co/X4Hzs1Mjxd https://t.co/PduLXQfYQN
Letitia Pinas led initiatives to improve her police unit’s ability to investigate suspects and identify and serve victims, boosting public trust in Suriname. #TIPReport https://t.co/w3pfMqx4P1

As a social worker and educator based in Barcelona, Rosa has led advocacy and institutional relations for SICARcat, the largest anti-trafficking NGO in the Catalonia region in Spain. #TIPReport https://t.co/BOmYIVLIJ1

Marijana Savić is an activist dedicated to advancing women's and girls' rights in Serbia, supporting recovery programs for survivors of trafficking and gender-based violence.  #TIPReport https://t.co/wbYowcPfNH
Samson Inocencio Jr. has dedicated over 20 years to combating human trafficking through his work with the International Justice Mission in the Philippines. #TIPReport https://t.co/7aWy7BLX0n
Oumou Elkhairou Niaré Samaké led the adoption of new anti-trafficking laws and fought to increase trafficking prosecutions in Mali. #TIPReport https://t.co/EgFMLoD6NX
Edith Murogo of Kenya has assisted over 50,000 domestic workers in gaining skills and knowledge to prevent them from becoming victims of trafficking. #TIPReport https://t.co/9T28uA0OeT
Mustafa al-Yasiri improved the Government of Iraq’s efforts to combat trafficking in persons and enhanced services for women trafficking victims. #TIPReport https://t.co/JJrxGm07hG

As the co-founder and Executive Director of Free Society Project, Maria Werlau researches, documents, and denounces exploitation and forced labor in Cuba’s labor export program. #TIPReport https://t.co/9O6VNejCyv

Marcela Martinez is a Bolivian lawyer and an anti-trafficking activist who helped shape national and regional efforts to combat trafficking in persons. From her law firm, she operates the #RedAlertTempranaZar hashtag to help activate searches for victims in Bolivia. #TIPReport https://t.co/SODGfC9lQQ

As the manager of a migration welfare center onsite at the Dhaka airport in Bangladesh, Al Amin Noyon has supported more than 34,000 Bangladeshi trafficking survivors and migrants. #TIPReport https://t.co/9lbCRl9TuG
Each year, the State Department recognizes individuals dedicated to ending human trafficking around the world. This year, we honor 10 heroes for their extraordinary work ⬇️ https://t.co/5oGKyQZJd9
https://t.co/W4ZAJ01FbT
@SecBlinken delivers remarks on the release of the 2024 Trafficking in Persons Report. https://t.co/W4ZAJ01FbT
Human trafficking is among the worst abuses that exist. We must each play a role in ending it. We cannot turn away. https://t.co/r4MsqRbAFY
This week at State: Summit on Peace in Ukraine; preparing for the NATO Summit; World Refugee Day; and announcing six new passport agencies. https://t.co/ogxKnz7rqr

Ahead of this summer’s games, #TeamUSA Paralympian Oksana Masters speaks about Ukrainian and American athletes supporting one another. The Olympic and Paralympic Games create positive impact for global communities through peaceful competition. #OneForAll https://t.co/HDz9ycwmui
“Pride is a time to reflect on all of the accomplishments that we’ve achieved for the security and rights of LGBTQI+ people''. — @US_SE_LGBTQI Stern #PrideMonth https://t.co/mjmZFZcrpQ

We’re celebrating all things #Pride this month. Do you know your history? Take our latest quiz to test your knowledge of LGBTQI+ U.S. milestones that shaped the movement and changed history. 🏳️‍🌈 https://t.co/KNvNr9O7xb

As we recognize Caribbean-American Heritage Month, @WHAAsstSecty Brian Nichols shares how, together, we continue to build a more prosperous Caribbean region as we work towards a hemisphere that reflects our shared values, goals, and commitment to democracy. https://t.co/sdALuZxHfD

2024 is on track to be one of the top five warmest years on record. It’s not too late. We are helping to limit the temperature increase to 1.5°C with the Global Methane Pledge. On #ShowYourStripes day, we are sharing our stripes to raise awareness about the Climate Crisis. https://t.co/az7eoaSXOr

At the heart of NATO is a commitment from each Ally to come to the defense of any other Ally. That means that a would-be aggressor knows that if they pick a fight with one, they pick a fight with all. https://t.co/2t1TgxSni5

Emma’s Torch in Washington, DC is one example of local community support that uplifts new refugees. Through culinary skills training and job placement support, refugees are being equipped for the workforce — and a new chapter of their lives in the United States. #worldrefugeeday https://t.co/05aGjrIbs0

On World Refugee Day, we give thanks to the Americans who volunteer their time and resources to help newly arrived refugees prepare for life in the U.S., including through our Welcome Corps initiative. Here’s how you can get involved: https://t.co/nXur79gnYp

On Juneteenth, marking the day the last slaves were freed in the U.S. in the aftermath of the Civil War, we reflect on our obligation to combat systemic racism. We must work together to realize the promise of freedom, liberty, and justice — for all people. https://t.co/YDv3TnZC6d

We are issuing more passports than ever before. Check your passport’s expiration date and apply early.

And to assist, today we announced plans to add 6 new passport agencies to better serve U.S. citizens traveling abroad. https://t.co/dfZCC2zwib https://t.co/Uf9b0Ig6ug

Just 21 days away from the Washington #NATOSummit, @SecBlinken met with @jensstoltenberg to discuss how the @NATO Alliance is ready to defend our future for the next 75 years and reaffirm our commitment to Ukraine. We look forward to welcoming leaders to Washington in July. https://t.co/PoNmZkk7ZS

🤝 A handshake communicates trust, respect, equality.

We asked @NATO Youth Summit participants what the Alliance’s handshake might look like. Check out what they came up with to highlight the strength and unity of NATO. https://t.co/LxeYEJU8ke
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with @NATO Secretary General Jens Stoltenberg. https://t.co/i9j0iKYvVd https://t.co/7OK2kz8Ls3
Our @NATO Alliance remains a bulwark of global security and stability — as it’s been for more than seven decades. https://t.co/tC3ZQeBQnu
Houthi attacks in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden have not only disrupted global shipping, but they’re also endangering the lives of innocent Yemenis. https://t.co/Dwrj1TwZ1z

This weekend, @VP Harris joined Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and leaders from 90+ countries to discuss a principled framework for peace in Ukraine. Participants reaffirmed support for Ukraine’s sovereignty, independence, and territorial integrity. #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/scYb0QcNCr

.@SecBlinken on @NATO: The purpose of this defensive Alliance is to allow us to get on with the real business of life, the real business of government, the real business of society: “achieving a fuller and happier life for all... our citizens.” https://t.co/4sBxb1ZMf1
Eid Mubarak to all commemorating Eid al-Adha around the world. https://t.co/im4BJE9Cnk

This week at State, @SecBlinken continued to push for a ceasefire in Gaza and announced new U.S. aid for Palestinians. Later he joined @POTUS in Italy for the @G7 Leaders Meeting. https://t.co/bhOzkHe811

.@G7 nations are rooted in shared beliefs in democratic principles and free societies, universal human rights, social progress, and respect for multilateralism and the rule of law. We seek to strengthen international rules and norms for the benefit of all. https://t.co/bkeFsi5VEy

Our goal is to strengthen Ukraine’s credible defense and deterrence capabilities for the long term. A lasting peace for Ukraine must be underwritten by Ukraine’s own ability to defend itself now and to deter future aggression anytime in the future. - @POTUS https://t.co/3h8wNX3dC2

.@G7 leaders gathered in Apulia, Italy this week to reaffirm their enduring unity and determination to meet global challenges at a crucial moment in history and as the international community confronts multiple interconnected crises. https://t.co/bsazgTLwaR https://t.co/KGTNTEqMky

The U.S. and our @G7 partners want people everywhere to thrive. That means helping those in emerging markets develop what they need to participate in the global economy. And it's why the G7 launched the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment. https://t.co/kcHgrWZB9u

.@POTUS and Ukrainian President @zelenskyyua have signed a bilateral security agreement between the United States and Ukraine. The agreement will bolster Euro-Atlantic security, stability, and prosperity. https://t.co/vMVXZAcp5k

The U.S. and our @G7 partners want people everywhere to thrive. That means helping friends in emerging markets develop what they need to participate in the global economy. And its why we launched the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment. https://t.co/xlR5iRk9M0

The @G7 Summit gets underway in Italy’s Apulia region today.

President Biden will join other G7 Leaders to address global challenges including the conflict in Gaza—specifically ongoing efforts to secure a ceasefire deal—and the war in Ukraine. https://t.co/OtSCOWmrus
As we work urgently to achieve a ceasefire, it is critical to turn the ceasefire into an enduring end of war and build a just, durable peace. https://t.co/RweZUqBMpj

Yesterday, we announced an additional $404 million in lifesaving humanitarian aid to support Palestinians. We urge other donors to contribute to the humanitarian response and increase support to those affected by the conflict. https://t.co/891oRtGQtF

We want to make sure not only that more humanitarian assistance is getting to Gaza but that once it’s in Gaza, it’s getting effectively distributed to people who desperately need it. https://t.co/ssISFf6ahq
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Qatari Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani in Doha, Qatar. https://t.co/gk6Mps6GQk
We’re taking bold, immediate actions to help civilians in Gaza by announcing new aid for Palestinians and ensuring that more aid gets into Gaza to those who need it most. https://t.co/Ah72YKYt7u

Earlier today @SecBlinken announced $404 million in new aid to Palestinians, while also urging that now is the time for other countries to step up and give — or give more — to alleviate the suffering of Palestinian people in Gaza. Watch his full remarks: https://t.co/i7o7GvDBnP

The United States will continue our efforts every single day to secure a ceasefire in Gaza, to get hostages home, and to put Israelis and Palestinians alike on the path to more durable peace and security. https://t.co/vtoQUyvHqg

It remains absolutely essential that we get more aid to Palestinians who need it in Gaza, that that aid gets around and distributed to those who are so desperately in need of it, and that we continue to work on protecting civilians and get them out of harm’s way. https://t.co/emvTCGvVsQ

We are focused on a durable end to the war. One that brings all hostages home, ensures Israel’s security, creates a better “day after” in Gaza without Hamas in power, and sets the stage for a political settlement that provides a better future for Israelis and Palestinians alike. https://t.co/AMvUCgiOhM

Digital solidarity is about locking arms with partners and working together to create systems, policies, and norms to help us maximize the promise of digital technology and build guardrails to address its risks. @StateCDP’s Nathaniel Fick explains more. https://t.co/zJCnFIigr9
This week at State, @POTUS and @SecBlinken traveled to France to commemorate the 80th anniversary of D-Day and also celebrate the enduring relationship with our oldest ally. https://t.co/Gr7Tf0y0ma

During Pride Month, the vibrant colors of the pride flag illuminate our embassies around the world and our team shows their support through activities and celebrations. We reaffirm our commitment to advancing the human rights of #LGBTQI+ persons everywhere. https://t.co/hbyt74yxpL

The climate crisis threatens coral reefs. We recently caught up with a coral scientist in Miami who shared the importance of these living — and vital — organisms. We are committed to global efforts to protect our ocean. #WorldOceanDay https://t.co/LVbtFPMrZU

It's #WorldOceanDay! 🪸🪼 Through our initiatives and strategies we are committed to advancing marine protected areas and conserving 30 percent of the ocean by 2030. https://t.co/Uo2234Q2Fu https://t.co/EM2tBJMlNr

France is America’s oldest friend and ally. Our relationship is built upon our shared commitment to the same values – democracy, human rights, the rule of law, security, and prosperity. https://t.co/2MddtSSPMh
“Democracy begins with each of us.” - @POTUS at Pointe du Hoc, France https://t.co/2nfqNMpHB5

What the Allies did together 80 years ago far surpassed anything we could have done on our own. It was a powerful illustration of how alliances — real alliances — make us stronger — a lesson that I pray we Americans never forget. —@POTUS https://t.co/sGb6bEssRj

How did an immigrant fleeing Nazi persecution end up parachuting into France on D-Day and later joining the U.S. diplomatic ranks? In @NMADmuseum's Stories of Diplomacy, we detail the remarkable career of Karl Mautner: https://t.co/K24TXuLb4b

Les Schrenk, Jake Larson, and Frank Perry are just a few of the World War II veterans — now in their 90s and 100s — who return to France this week to commemorate D-Day's 80th anniversary. Learn about their stories and the trans-Atlantic bond they forged.
https://t.co/CtqgqksNCu

In honor of the 80th anniversary of D-Day, an eternal flame was brought from France to Arlington National Cemetery and the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, reenforcing the bond between our peoples and the sacrifices made for our collective freedom. #DDay80 https://t.co/cTxN9AQzzE

As we commemorate 80 years since the D-Day operation, we honor the the courage, bravery, and sacrifice of U.S. and Allied forces. Crack the code to uncover the heroes of World War 2: https://t.co/9YtmvcIeb2 https://t.co/Lhu7TngZNq

On the 35th anniversary of the Tiananmen Square massacre, the U.S. stands in solidarity with those who continue the struggle for human rights and individual freedom. The courage and sacrifice of the people who stood up in Tiananmen Square will not be forgotten. https://t.co/8ynMMxaPsh

Later this week, @POTUS will commemorate the 80th anniversary of D-Day and participate in an official state visit with President Macron in France. @WhiteHouse’s John Kirby shares details with @ForeignPressCtr reporters about the upcoming visit. https://t.co/KtSqF83RCD
The G7 stands behind the comprehensive proposal that would lead to an immediate ceasefire in Gaza. https://t.co/WQD7hjOWL6
It is time to finalize this agreement, put an end to the violence, and build a better future for both Palestinians and Israelis alike. https://t.co/Tow29tfDbu

We are focused on a durable end to the war. One that brings all hostages home, ensures Israel’s security, creates a better “day after” in Gaza without Hamas in power, and sets the stage for a political settlement that provides a better future for Israelis and Palestinians alike. https://t.co/NhRp3fBzLs
The world should know, the Palestinian people should know that the only thing standing in the way of an immediate ceasefire today is Hamas. https://t.co/iE19szDaUH
June is Caribbean American Heritage Month, a time to recognize the many contributions that the community has made and continues to make to America’s cultural diversity. https://t.co/FJGUdwxEEh

Hurricanes and tropical storms can ruin a vacation quickly, and even become life threatening. As we head into the Atlantic hurricane season, which also overlaps with summer travel, these seven tips from @TravelGov will help keep you safe. https://t.co/fz36nl7Ds0

This week at State, @SecBlinken traveled to Moldova and Czechia to coordinate with partners on a shared response to Russian aggression in Ukraine. While in Prague he also joined NATO Foreign Ministers to discuss the upcoming NATO Summit in Washington. https://t.co/UR497Gq6yH

Most Americans have their own immigration story of relatives and ancestors who overcame incredible odds to build new lives and contribute to the fabric of America. During National Immigrant Heritage Month, we celebrate the contributions and achievements of immigrants in America. https://t.co/PvkLGo4cZ9

Despite great strides for equality around the world, LGBTQI+ persons in many countries continue to face grave danger. Protecting the spirit of #Pride and promoting respect for the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons is central to the work we do. https://t.co/vN4Sz19a2Y
